senses.definition: to move at a moderate, three-beat gait slower than a gallop, typical of a horse
examples: The horse cantered across the field.
She cantered gently around the arena.

senses.definition: relating to or resembling Canterbury or its cathedral; historically used to describe someone from Canterbury
examples: The canterburian streets were crowded with visitors.
A canterburian tradition is still celebrated there.

senses.definition: the qualities or characteristics associated with Canterbury or its culture; a historically rare term
examples: Scholars discussed canterburianism in medieval studies.
The text shows canterburianism in its references to cathedral life.

senses.definition: plural form; sites or items associated with Canterbury; could be used historically or humorously
examples: The canterburies held old manuscripts.
They explored the canterburies of the old town.

senses.definition: a city in Kent, England, known for its cathedral; also a style of spelling for the keyboarding layout? (common sense: the cathedral city)
examples: I visited Canterbury to see the cathedral.
Canterbury is famous for its historic buildings.

senses.definition: plural of Canterbury; refers to multiple Canterbury-related items or places
examples: They toured several canterburys in the region.
The canterburys on the map show historical sites.

senses.definition: moved in the canter (past tense)
examples: The horse cantered past the fence.
She cantered along the path after the ride.

senses.definition: a type of edible mushroom, also called chanterelle; a slightly distorted/alternate spelling
examples: We cooked canterelles with butter.
Fresh canterelles are highly prized by chefs.

senses.definition: a person who canters; a rider who moves at a canter
examples: The canterer guided the horse along the arena.
A skilled canterer maintains a smooth gait.

senses.definition: the action of moving at a steady canter
examples: The horse was cantering around the ring.
He enjoys cantering on the countryside trails.

senses.definition: plural of canter; multiple instances of the gait or riders who ride at a canter
examples: The horses broke into canters across the field.
Several canters joined the session today.

senses.definition: obsolete or rare form; not commonly used in modern English
examples: A cantest of heraldic terms appeared in the manuscript.

senses.definition: relating to the angle between the eye socket and the nose; canthal is a medical/ anatomical term
examples: Canthal distance is used in facial measurements.
The canthal area was swelling after the injury.

senses.definition: a genus of fungi in the chanterelle family
examples: He studied cantharellus species in the forest.
Cantharellus mushrooms are edible.

senses.definition: a plural or variant form related to cantharid beetles or compounds; context-specific
examples: Notes described canthari in the beetle family.

senses.definition: of or relating to cantharides (a type of blistering agent) or cantharidin
examples: The cantharic toxin was studied historically in medicine.

senses.definition: a blistering agent produced by blister beetles; cantharidin
examples: Cantharid was once used in medicine but is highly toxic.
Exposure to cantharid can cause skin blisters.

senses.definition: the family of blister beetles; cantharidae
examples: Cantharidae is a large family of beetles known for producing cantharidin.
Researchers study the Cantharidae to understand blister beetle behavior.

senses.definition: relating to cantharid or cantharidin
examples: Cantharidal preparations were used historically in medicine.

senses.definition: to treat or convert with cantharid; to induce cantharid poisoning in archaic language
examples: Historical texts describe attempts to cantharidate substances.

senses.definition: poisoned or treated with cantharidin; contaminated with cantharid
examples: Water was cantharidated in the old experiments.

senses.definition: the act of applying cantharidin to something; now obsolete
examples: The manuscript mentions cantharidating techniques.

senses.definition: relating to blister beetles or cantharidin; belonging to or resembling this group
examples: Cantharidean features were noted in the beetles.

senses.definition: the genus or group of blister beetles; or cantharides (the substance cantharidin) historically used as a medicine
examples: Blister beetles of cantharides produce cantharidin.
Historically, cantharides was used as a medicine.

senses.definition: relating to cantharides or blister beetles; of the cantharidan family
examples: Cantharidian specimens were catalogued in the collection.
